executive_summary: Iteration 000004 is largely successful: five of six user stories (US-002 through US-006) are fully implemented and conform to all acceptance criteria. The single non-conforming area is US-001 (Bun build pipeline), where the build script to produce three cross-platform standalone binaries (linux-x64, darwin-x64, win32-x64) was never implemented. No package.json build script, bunfig.toml, or equivalent bundling configuration exists. The CLI is fully functional when invoked via `node cli/zikon.js`, but the distributable binary artifact goal stated in FR-1 and US-001-AC01 through AC04 is unmet. All other functional requirements (FR-2 through FR-9) are satisfied, including default and custom installation paths, GPU detection with CUDA/ROCm/MPS/CPU backend selection, runtime dependency validation with actionable error messages, correct stderr/stdout channel separation, idempotent shell profile updates on Linux/macOS, Windows PATH instructions, and comprehensive README documentation.

verification_by_fr.note: No build script exists to target linux-x64, darwin-x64, or win32-x64. No bunfig.toml, no 'build' entry in package.json. Only a 'test' script is present.
get_install_dir() returns ~/.zikon (Linux/macOS) via HOME or os.homedir(), and %USERPROFILE%\.zikon on Windows. Verified in cli/zikon.js lines 72-81.

parse_install_arguments() handles both --installation-path=<path> and --installation-path <path> forms. Custom path completely replaces default; no fallback to ~/.zikon. Verified in cli/zikon.js lines 83-104, 574.

detect_nvidia_gpu(), detect_amd_gpu(), detect_apple_silicon_gpu() cover all three vendors. Absence of supported GPU returns { vendor: 'none' } and falls back to CPU-only without crashing. Verified in cli/zikon.js lines 210-386.

validate_install_dependencies() runs at lines 565-571, before install_runtime() (which contains all filesystem writes) is called at line 577. Missing tools cause immediate exit(1) with no directory creation.
All installer/progress messages use write_stderr(). stdout is only written at the end of the generate flow via process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(result)). Install flow produces no stdout output.
Constants defined at lines 27-30: EXIT_SUCCESS=0, EXIT_GENERATION_ERROR=1, EXIT_TRACE_ERROR=2, EXIT_INVALID_ARGUMENTS=3. All install code paths use the correct constant.

ensure_profile_path_entry() appends a marked block (# >>> zikon >>> / # <<< zikon <<<) to ~/.bashrc and ~/.zshrc. Idempotency is enforced by checking for the marker before writing. Runs only on non-Windows platforms.
On win32, installer prints 'Add <bin_dir> to PATH manually, then open a new terminal.' and returns immediately without touching registry or any shell config. Verified in cli/zikon.js lines 512-514.

verification_by_us.note: AC01: No three-binary build exists. AC02: No bundling of cli/zikon.js with Node deps. AC03: No embedding of scripts/ as build artifact. AC04: Build cannot complete — no build script. AC05: Cannot assess lint/typecheck in the context of a build pipeline that does not exist.

AC01: Directory created with mkdirSync recursive. AC02: cli/, scripts/generate/, scripts/trace/ all copied. AC03: uv sync executed for Python deps. AC04: npm/pnpm install executed for tracer Node deps. AC05: Functional when run via node — binary prerequisite from US-001 is the blocker for this to work from a downloaded binary. AC06: ~/.bashrc and ~/.zshrc updated idempotently via marked blocks. AC07: Windows prints manual PATH instructions. AC08: Manual cross-platform verification is inherently out-of-scope for code audit.

AC01: --installation-path creates and populates custom directory with same layout. AC02: No files written to ~/.zikon when flag is provided. AC03: Functional after install at custom path. AC04: ensure_writable_install_dir() throws with clear message on write failure; caught and rethrown as exit(1). AC05: Manual cross-platform verification out-of-scope for code audit.

AC01: All three vendors detected (NVIDIA via nvidia-smi, AMD via rocm-smi, Apple via sysctl+system_profiler). AC02: NVIDIA ≥4GB → cuda. AC03: AMD ≥4GB → rocm. AC04: Apple Silicon arm64 → mps. AC05: No GPU or <4GB VRAM → cpu with printed warning. AC06: GPU name, VRAM, vendor, and backend printed to stderr. AC07: Manual multi-machine verification out-of-scope.

AC01: bun, node, npm/pnpm all checked. AC02: uv checked. AC03: Each missing tool prints actionable message with install URL/command; exits non-zero before any filesystem change. AC04: All tools present → proceeds without prompts. AC05: Manual verification with missing tool out-of-scope.

AC01: README.md has an 'Installation' section covering Linux, macOS, and Windows. AC02: Prerequisites table lists Bun, Node.js, uv with install links for each OS. AC03: Exact command sequences from clone to first zikon invocation provided for Bash and PowerShell. AC04: Visual browser verification checklist included in README.

minor_observations: The README installation commands use `node cli/zikon.js install` rather than a downloaded binary path — this is consistent with the current state of the project (no build pipeline), but once US-001 is implemented, the README will need updating to reflect binary download and usage.

ensure_profile_path_entry() updates both ~/.bashrc AND ~/.zshrc unconditionally on all non-Windows platforms, even if one or both files do not exist. A missing file will be created by fs.writeFileSync. This is intentional but could be surprising if the user's shell is not bash or zsh (e.g., fish).

The runtime dependency check exits with EXIT_GENERATION_ERROR (code 1) when dependencies are missing, rather than EXIT_INVALID_ARGUMENTS (code 3). While exit code 1 is technically correct for a pre-condition failure, a 'missing dependency' could arguably map to 'environment setup error' rather than 'generation error'. This is a minor naming/semantic concern with the current constant naming.

The Apple Silicon GPU detection reads system memory (RAM) via system_profiler and uses it as vram_gb proxy. On Apple Silicon, RAM is unified memory shared with the GPU — this is correct technically, but it may report much more than the actual GPU budget for typical inference workloads.
No test exercises the full end-to-end install flow on a real filesystem (all tests use stubs/mocks). This is acceptable for unit testing but leaves integration coverage absent.

The Windows shim (zikon.cmd) hardcodes a relative path (`%~dp0\..\cli\zikon.js`) that assumes the install layout is always bin/../cli/zikon.js. This is correct for the current layout but may break if the bin directory is relocated.
conclusions_and_recommendations: The implementation of iteration 000004 is strong overall with one critical gap: the Bun build pipeline (US-001 / FR-1) was not implemented. The stated goal of shipping Zikon as a self-contained distributable downloaded and run with a single command cannot be achieved until binaries are produced. The following actions are recommended: (1) CRITICAL — Implement the Bun build script: add a 'build' entry to package.json that calls `bun build --compile --target=bun-linux-x64 --outfile=dist/zikon-linux cli/zikon.js` (and equivalent for macos/windows), embed or co-locate the scripts/ directory as documented in US-001-AC03, and validate that the build completes cleanly. (2) MINOR — Update README installation commands to reference the downloaded binary paths once the build pipeline exists. (3) MINOR — Consider adding a note in shell profile update logic about non-bash/non-zsh shells (fish, nushell, etc.). (4) MINOR — Revisit the exit code used for missing runtime dependencies; EXIT_PRECONDITION_FAILED (a new code or reusing 3) could be more semantically accurate, though the current behavior is not incorrect.
